title: Editor’s picks · the weekend of June 26–28

events.title: Shakespeare’s ‘Much Ado About Nothing’ at Legacy Square
events.loc: Legacy Square · 510 S. Third St., Renton
events.desc: An abridged, family-friendly take on the classic comedy — soldiers, maidens, matchmaking and mischief — staged at the brand-new Legacy Square park.

events.title: The Taste Northwest
events.loc: MattressFirm Showplex · 110 Ninth Ave. S.W., Puyallup
events.desc: Sample $6 bites from a range of vendors with live music. New this year: corgi races benefiting Racing to End Alzheimer’s.

events.title: Arts in Nature Festival
events.desc: A day at 68-acre Camp Long with forest trails, a meadow, onstage performances, interactive art installations and dedicated kids’ art zones.

events.title: Mukilteo Touch a Truck
events.loc: Mukilteo Lighthouse Park · 609 Front St., Mukilteo
events.desc: Kids climb into the driver’s seat of working trucks and vehicles at a hidden-gem lighthouse park, with special activities and free swag.

events.title: Seafair Pirates Landing
events.loc: Alki Beach Park · 2701 Alki Ave. S.W., Seattle
events.desc: A 70-year-old tradition: watch for the tugboat, then the Seafair Pirates storm Alki Beach in search of the key to the city. Meet the new captain and crew — and catch a pirate story time beforehand.

events.title: Sally’s Day at The Museum of Flight
events.cost: With admission ($21–$29; ages 4 & under free) or membership
events.loc: The Museum of Flight · 9404 E. Marginal Way S., Seattle
events.desc: A day honoring Sally Ride, the first American woman in space, with a spacesuit-design fashion show and a talk from a Blue Origin astronaut director.

events.title: Woodinville Welcomes the World: Watch Party
events.loc: Wilmot Gateway Park · 17301 131st Ave. N.E., Woodinville
events.desc: A World Cup watch party on a giant screen showing three matches, with food trucks, local wine and beer, and lawn-chair seating.

events.title: Slug Fest
events.cost: With admission ($17–$30; ages 1 & under free) or membership
events.loc: Northwest Trek Wildlife Park · 11610 Trek Dr., Eatonville
events.desc: Craft your own tentacles and slip through a human slug race while learning why slugs matter to the ecosystem — plus all the usual wildlife-park animals.

events.title: Skandia Midsommarfest
events.cost: Free; Discover Pass required for parking
events.loc: Saint Edward State Park · 14445 Juanita Dr. N.E., Kenmore
events.desc: A Midsummer celebration with a parade and pole-raising, flower-crown making, local vendors, a food truck and a great playground.

events.title: Seattle Pride Parade
events.loc: Downtown Seattle · 4th Ave. & Pike St., Seattle
events.desc: Washington’s largest parade, with 300,000+ expected, marching from downtown to Seattle Center — followed by the month’s biggest Pride party.
